comment_text.sn35.130:1.7: For the syntax of this sentence, see Bodhi’s note in Connected Discourses.

root_text.sn35.130:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahākaccāno avantīsu viharati kuraraghare papāte pabbate.
root_text.sn35.130:1.2: Atha kho hāliddikāni gahapati yenāyasmā mahākaccāno tenupasaṅkami …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho hāliddikāni gahapati āyasmantaṁ mahākaccānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.130:1.4: ‘dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ; phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati vedanānānattan’ti.
root_text.sn35.130:1.5: Kathaṁ nu kho, bhante, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ; phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati vedanānānattan”ti?
root_text.sn35.130:1.6: “Idha, gahapati, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā ‘manāpaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti cakkhuviññāṇaṁ sukhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:1.8: Cakkhunā kho paneva rūpaṁ disvā ‘amanāpaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti cakkhuviññāṇaṁ dukkhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:1.10: Cakkhunā kho paneva rūpaṁ disvā ‘upekkhāṭṭhāniyaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti cakkhuviññāṇaṁ adukkhamasukhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapati, bhikkhu sotena saddaṁ sutvā …pe…
root_text.sn35.130:2.5: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya ‘manāpaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti manoviññāṇaṁ sukhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:2.7: Manasā kho paneva dhammaṁ viññāya ‘amanāpaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti manoviññāṇaṁ dukkhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:2.9: Manasā kho paneva dhammaṁ viññāya ‘upekkhāṭṭhāniyaṁ itthetan’ti pajānāti manoviññāṇaṁ adukkhamasukhavedaniyañca.
root_text.sn35.130:2.11: Evaṁ kho, gahapati, dhātunānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati phassanānattaṁ; phassanānattaṁ paṭicca uppajjati vedanānānattan”ti.

translation_text.sn35.130:1.1: At one time Venerable Mahākaccāna was staying in the land of the Avantis near Kuraraghara on Steep Mountain.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.2: Then the householder Hāliddikāni went up to Venerable Mahākaccāna … and asked him,
translation_text.sn35.130:1.3: “Sir, this was said by the Buddha:
translation_text.sn35.130:1.4: ‘Diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts, and diversity of contacts gives rise to diversity of feelings.’
translation_text.sn35.130:1.5: How does diversity of elements give rise to diversity of contacts, and diversity of contacts gives rise to diversity of feelings?”
translation_text.sn35.130:1.6: “Householder, it’s when a mendicant sees a sight with the eye and understands it to be agreeable.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.7: There is eye consciousness; and pleasant feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as pleasant.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.8: Then they see a sight and understand it to be disagreeable.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.9: There is eye consciousness; and painful feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as painful.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.10: Then they see a sight and understand it to be a basis for equanimity.
translation_text.sn35.130:1.11: There is eye consciousness; and neutral feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as neutral.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant hears a sound with the ear …
translation_text.sn35.130:2.2: smells an odor with the nose …
translation_text.sn35.130:2.3: tastes a flavor with the tongue …
translation_text.sn35.130:2.4: feels a touch with the body …
translation_text.sn35.130:2.5: knows an idea with the mind and understands it to be agreeable.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.6: There is mind consciousness; and pleasant feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as pleasant.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.7: Then they know an idea and understand it to be disagreeable.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.8: There is mind consciousness; and painful feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as painful.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.9: Then they know an idea and understand it to be a basis for equanimity.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.10: Neutral feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as neutral.
translation_text.sn35.130:2.11: That’s how diversity of elements gives rise to diversity of contacts, and diversity of contacts gives rise to diversity of feelings.”

variant_text.sn35.130:1.1: kuraraghare → kulaghare (sya-all, mr) | papāte → pavatte (bj, pts1ed); sampavatte (sya-all, km, mr)
variant_text.sn35.130:1.6: sukhavedaniyañca → sukhavedanīyañca (bj); sukhavedanīyaṁ (bj); sukhavedaniyaṁ (sya-all, km, mr); sukhavedaniyaṁ (pts1ed)
